title: phyllactis pratensis
side_effects: Phyllactis pratensis can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high concentration of toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to the plant's extracts can also lead to allergic reactions, such as skin irritation, itching, and rashes. In severe cases, improper use of Phyllactis pratensis can even cause respiratory problems, including coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath. Furthermore, it can interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners, and exacerbate underlying health conditions.
